after a year of doing weekly launches you're going to have 52 different lists. So that can get a little cumbersome. There is, as far as members platforms, if you're doing like a membership site, like, you know, the way Product Launch Formula is a membership site, you know, we've got a portal for Product Launch Formula. If you're doing a portal, you want to look for software that allows you to basically drip, that's what they call it, drip out content. Now, if you're doing a portal or a membership site where they get all the content in it immediately when they join, then you don't have to worry about it. And then finally with staffing, you know, one of the beautiful things with the Evergreen is that you smooth out a lot of your lead flows, your sales. So it makes it easier to plan your customer service if you get an X number of sales every week instead of like, you know, 500 sales and then no sales for like a year and then 500 more sales. So Evergreen is nice in terms of the load on your server and the load on your customer service, the load on your tech support, anything like that, it can be good. You don't want to screw up and mail, you know, when they're supposed to get the first piece of pre-launch content, mail them to the sales letter or something like that.
